issue_title: Cert Management Improvement Project (C-MIP)
issue_body:
Based on previous discussions (#3587, #2071, #338), we should have an improved overall strategy for managing certs. This looks something like:

Step 1. list-certs command
Step 2. Specify canonical `{archive, live}` directories in conf file
Step 3. --use-lineage-name <lineage-name>
Step 4. --set-lineage-name <lineage-name>
Step 5. --use-lineage-name <wrong name or domain names or whatever>, "did you mean" based on filenames and such if ambiguous

Discussion items:
A. What should we call the canonical selector that I have temporarily termed "lineage-name"?
B. Where does this canonical name live?
Options for this include:
- as a line item in the conf file, whose name does not matter
- as the filename of the conf file, and document this choice.

I am personally for the latter, based on @schoen's observation that the file system then ensures uniqueness.

cc: @pde @bmw @cowlicks @PaulSD @yaegashi

issue_title: Ssl-Cert
standalone plugin does not respect new --preferred-challenges flag
issue_body:
The standalone plugin will no longer respect the new --preferred-challenges flag.  

this can easily be seen for instance by starting a process that will occupy port 443, and limiting preferred challenges to challenges that will not use that port: 
This works fine:
certbot-auto certonly --force-renewal --standalone --standalone-supported-challenges http-01
But this new syntax will bomb out
certbot-auto certonly --force-renewal --standalone --preferred-challenges http-01
with "MisconfigurationError: At least one of the (possibly) required ports is already taken."  .. because it tests 443 despite being told not to.  
This is probably just a problem in the commandline parsing somewhere, or a function referring back the old config values.

issue_title: Temporary files/directory problem with FICO-Xpress in Windows
issue_body:
Hi all,

I'm opening a new issue, since I identified the source of the problem mentioned [here](https://github.com/coin-or/pulp/issues/143).

I'm using a Windows 7 Pro 64-bit system where I have installed FICO-Xpress 8.2, Python 3.5 and PuLP 1.6.5. In order to solve the optimisation problem with FICO-Xpress, PuLP has to
1. create a `.lp` file,
2. execute `optimizer.exe` to solve it and
3. read the `.prt` to retrieve the solution.

However this doesn't work in Windows because it seems that PuLP doesn't manage properly the name of the temporary directories and filenames with spaces. At least that was the case for me. So, I had to activate the `keepFiles` option and use a simple name for my problem, since this name is also used to create the `.lp` and `.prt` files.

Another thing that I had to do was to activate the `msg` option. I have no idea why, but when it isn't activated, PuLP doesn't work.

To summarize :
1. the name of the problem has to be simple, e.g. `prob = LpProblem("TheWhiskasProblem",LpMinimize)` and
2. the msg and keepFiles options have to be activated : `prob.setSolver(XPRESS(msg=1, keepFiles=True))`.

I hope that works for you too.

issue_title: Casperjs notebook/save.js skipping some asserts
issue_body:
While working on my own js tests, I ran into something strange. It seems that calling ``open_dashboard`` in a JS test causes casperjs to silently skip all following steps.

This can be seen in travis builds. For example in the [latest one]( https://travis-ci.org/jupyter/notebook/jobs/115562243#L1294), the ``notebook/save.js`` tests gives the following output :

```
Test file: /home/travis/build/jupyter/notebook/notebook/tests/notebook/save.js  
PASS Save did not fail
PASS Save OK
PASS Save with complicated name
PASS path OK
PASS checkpoints OK
Test file: /home/travis/build/jupyter/notebook/notebook/tests/notebook/shutdown.js 
```

But if you look at the [test code](https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/master/notebook/tests/notebook/save.js#L75), you see that there are two more asserts following the ``checkpoints OK`` one. The full test should print ``Escaped URL in notebook list`` and ``Notebook name is correct``. To confirm, if you add ``this.test.assert(false);`` in the last ``then``, you'll still get a passing test.

I can reproduce the same behavior on my OSX machine with phantomjs=2.1.1 and casper=1.1.0-beta5.

I tried debugging by adding some logging at the beginning of the ``notebook/save.js`` file :
```
    casper.on('remote.message', function(message) {
        this.echo('remote message caught: ' + message);
    });

    casper.options.onStepComplete = function(casper, result) {
        console.log('step complete ' + result);
    };
```

You can see the full output in [this gist](https://gist.github.com/julienr/1f7c3a953c5a418d7a95) : I get the following error printed :
```
remote message caught: Object {
  "readyState": 4,
  "responseText": "{\"reason\": null, \"message\": \"No such file or directory: has#hash and space and unic\\u00f8\\u2202e.ipynb\"}",
  "responseJSON":
    "reason": null,
    "message": "No such file or directory: has#hash and space and unicø∂e.ipynb"
  "status": 404,
  "statusText": "Not Found"
}
```

If I run with an external notebook server (using ``--url=http://localhost:8889``), I get some websocket errors with status code 426 as you can see in the second log of the gist. I am not sure if this is related.

I am quite new to phantomjs & casperjs, so I'm not really sure what's going on. Ideas welcome :-)

issue_title: How to auto close brackets and quotes in .py and other text file.
issue_body:
Hello!

I opened .py file in Jupyter Notebook. I type `prit(` and expect that bracket will close automatically. But it is missed. Other case: I selected some text in .py file, for example `x1`, and pressed `"` (double quote). My text is removed and double quote is typed.

I know that codemirror is used in Jupyter, but how to set up it?

Best regards, Andrey.

issue_title: mozdownload is no longer able to download builds from archive.mozilla.org
issue_body:
Via [bug 1211732](https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1211732) the files on archive.mozilla.org have been moved to S3. That means the HTML is different now and mozdownload fails to download any build.

As a quick workaround we can change the BASE_URL to `http://ftp-origin-scl3.mozilla.org/` to keep mozdownload working. 

I will get this fixed in a bit.

@xabolcs maybe that is what you were seeing?

issue_title: OS X 10.10 Install Problems (Using Homebrew Python)
issue_body:
I'm having problems installing nupic on a new OS X Yosemite mac.
It appears to install correctly but then trying to import the bindings crashes python.

    >>> import nupic.bindings.math
    Fatal Python error: PyThreadState_Get: no current thread
    Abort trap: 6
I'm using homebrew's version of python (v2.7.9), not the system version (v2.7.6).
Here's the link to the mailing list thread http://lists.numenta.org/pipermail/nupic_lists.numenta.org/2015-January/010230.html

Here’s how I installed everything (on a new mac):
 1. I installed XCode from the app store.
 - I installed home-brew in the standard way
````ruby -e "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/master/install)"````
 - installed python 2.7.9 `homebrew install python`
 - installed virtualenv `pip install virtualenv`
 - set up a virtual env in called "all" in ~/dev/ `virtualenv all`
 - installed nupic dependencies `brew install automake libtool cmake`
 - activated my virtualenv `source all/bin/activate`
 - installed NuPIC `pip install nupic`

It seemed to install OK. But I get the error above.

 - I have tried cloning the nupic repo and installing from that, but it gave the same error.
 - I also tried cloning the nupic.core repo and installing that as well. But again the same error.
 - Running `which python` outside of a virtualenv gives `/usr/local/bin/python` which is homebrew's python.

#### Some Further Investigating:

I found the point at which python crashes trying to run `import nupic.bindings.math` is when it tries to import a file called `_math.so`. (a copy is in `$NUPIC/nupic/bindings/` after install)
Looking at the console error log thing there is a reference to two versions of python, one is Homebrew's the other is Apple's.
I think the problem is that when _math.so is compiled it's somehow linked to the wrong version of python.

    $ otool -L $NUPIC/nupic/bindings/_math.so 
    ~/dev/nupic/nupic/bindings/_math.so:
        /usr/lib/libSystem.B.dylib (compatibility version 1.0.0, current version 1213.0.0)
        /System/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/Python (compatibility version 2.7.0, current version 2.7.6)
        /usr/lib/libc++.1.dylib (compatibility version 1.0.0, current version 120.0.0)

Looking at the output whilst nupic installs I did spot some references to non Homebrew python.
for example:

    building 'nupic.bindings._math' extension
    clang -fno-strict-aliasing -fno-common -dynamic -I/usr/local/include -I/usr/local/opt/sqlite/include -isysroot /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Platforms/MacOSX.platform/Developer/SDKs/MacOSX10.10.sdk -I/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Platforms/MacOSX.platform/Developer/SDKs/MacOSX10.10.sdk/System/Library/Frameworks/Tk.framework/Versions/8.5/Headers -DNDEBUG -g -fwrapv -O3 -Wall -Wstrict-prototypes -arch x86_64 -DNUPIC2 -DNTA_PLATFORM_darwin64 -DNTA_PYTHON_SUPPORT=2.7 -DNTA_INTERNAL -DNTA_ASSERTIONS_ON -DNTA_ASM -DHAVE_CONFIG_H -DBOOST_NO_WREGEX -I~/dev/nupic/external/darwin64/include -I~/dev/nupic/external/common/include -I~/dev/nupic/extensions -I~/dev/nupic -I~/dev/nupic.core/build/release/include -I~/dev/test/bin/../include/python2.7 -I~/dev/test/lib/python2.7/site-packages/numpy/core/include -I/usr/local/Cellar/python/2.7.9/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/include/python2.7 -c extensions/py_support/NumpyVector.cpp -o build/temp.macosx-10.10-x86_64-2.7/extensions/py_support/NumpyVector.o -std=c++11 -m64 -fPIC -fvisibility=hidden -Wall -Wreturn-type -Wunused -Wno-unused-parameter -stdlib=libc++

Apparently -isysroot is set in the python.rb formula for Homebrew:
https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew/blob/master/Library/Formula/python.rb#L213

I've found a few references to similar problems online. Unfortunately none of them have any definitive answers short of replacing the system's python.
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/20003268/impose-linking-to-specific-python-library-on-osx-lion
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/22649379/swig-generated-code-links-to-wrong-python-installation
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/2020228/swig-python-bindings-for-opencv-2-0-with-macports

issue_title: Port python TM to C++, using C++ Connections data structure
issue_labels: [{'id': 119506192, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/numenta/nupic.core/labels/priority:2', 'name': 'priority:2', 'color': 'eb6420', 'default': False}, {'id': 106562956, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/numenta/nupic.core/labels/status:in%20progress', 'name': 'status:in progress', 'color': 'bfe5bf', 'default': False}, {'id': 78895658, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/numenta/nupic.core/labels/subject:algorithms', 'name': 'subject:algorithms', 'color': 'c7def8', 'default': False}, {'id': 128692784, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/numenta/nupic.core/labels/super', 'name': 'super', 'color': 'bfdadc', 'default': False}, {'id': 77738351, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/numenta/nupic.core/labels/type:enhancement', 'name': 'type:enhancement', 'color': '006b75', 'default': False}, {'id': 378142579, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/numenta/nupic.core/labels/under-review', 'name': 'under-review', 'color': 'fbca04', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
The python temporal memory implementation is located [here](https://github.com/numenta/nupic/blob/master/nupic/research/temporal_memory.py), and a subclass of it that uses the C++ Connections data structure is located [here](https://github.com/numenta/nupic/blob/master/nupic/research/fast_temporal_memory.py). Unit tests are located [here](https://github.com/numenta/nupic/blob/master/tests/unit/nupic/research/temporal_memory_test.py).

This task is to port the above implementation to C++, so that we have a pure C++ version that uses the Connections data structure.

---
- [x] [Create logical C++ API](https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/issues/411)
- [x] [Map C++ API to Python API in SWiG](https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/issues/414)
- [x] [Port existing Python unit tests to C++](https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/issues/415)
- [x] [Ensure random number generation is consistent between Python / C++](https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/issues/416)
- [x] [Implement Capnp serialization configuration for TM](https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/issues/418)
- [ ] [Run exact same python test code run against C++ TM algorithm](https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/issues/419)
- [x] [Investigate optimizations on C++ TM code](https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/issues/417)
- [x] [Add TM constructor parameter checks](https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/issues/504)
- [x] [TM unit test testWrite function completion](https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/issues/505)

issue_title: 在使用了ActionBar的页面，调用xpath相关接口报错
issue_body:
## Versions (版本)
0.1.3dev9

## Issue and steps to reproduce (复现步骤)
已安装lxml，版本为4.2.3
1. 在使用了'android.support.v7.app.ActionBar'的安卓App页面
2. 调用任意xpath相关接口，如: 
`self.driver.xpath("//*[@text='Test']").wait(5.0)`
3. 报错，错误为
`Invalid tag name 'android.support.v7.app.ActionBar$Tab'`

## 分析
在`simplexml.py`中
21行开始：
```
        root = etree.fromstring(xml_content.encode('utf-8'))
        for node in root.xpath("//node"):
            node.tag = node.attrib.pop("class")
        return root.xpath(xpath)
```
 当遇到类似
`class="android.support.v7.app.ActionBar$Tab"`
这种值时，由于包含`$`的值不能作为tag，触发上述错误，注释掉`node.tag = node.attrib.pop("class")`后似乎并不影响xpath相关功能的使用。

这里这样修改tag的目的是什么呢？如果目前不需要的话，可以先去掉这段，避免类似错误，或者处理一下class的值？

issue_title: I2C OSError: Success
issue_body:
I've been trying to get communication working over I2C between our roboRIO and an Arduino, and I've run into some issues.  It appears the Arduino (sometimes?) successfully receives data, but regardless of whether or not the data goes through an error is thrown on the roboRIO side.  

Here's the code:
```python
self.arduinoC = wpilib.I2C(wpilib.I2C.Port.kOnboard, 4)
self.arduinoC.transaction(b'sbt255', 1)
```
(docs for I2C are [here](http://robotpy.readthedocs.io/projects/wpilib/en/stable/wpilib/I2C.html?highlight=i2c#wpilib.i2c.I2C.transaction))


And here's the result of local tests:
```python
self = <hal_impl.i2c_helpers.I2CSimBase object at 0x10dc90d68>, port = 0, deviceAddress = 4, dataToSend = <hal.functions.c_ubyte_Array_6 object at 0x10dc6f488>, sendSize = 6
dataReceived = <hal.functions.c_ubyte_Array_1 object at 0x10dc6fbf8>, receiveSize = 1

    def transactionI2C(self, port, deviceAddress, dataToSend, sendSize, dataReceived, receiveSize):
        '''
                To give data back use ``data_received``::
    
                    data_received[:] = [1,2,3...]
    
                :returns: number of bytes returned
            '''
>       raise NotImplementedError
E       NotImplementedError
```

I'm guessing this is the result of 2018 changes to WPILib, but I'm not familiar enough with it to confirm.

   when rendered on GitHub might be closed with a friendly reminder of this.

Also, add steps to reproduce the bug, if applicable. Sample code would be nice too :)

For more information on how to submit valuable contributions,
see https://opensource.guide/how-to-contribute/#how-to-submit-a-contribution
-->
## Problem
S3Client implementation of config options prevents the use of `ConfigParser`'s `[DEFAULT]` section.

Due to the implementation of `luigi/contrib/s3.py`'s auto-fill options using all key-value pairs existing in the `[s3]` section within one's specified luigi configuration file, errors are thrown when attempting to utilize Python's ConfigParser `[DEFAULT]` section.

Functionality appears to have been added in PR #500

### Presence of Bug
From what i can tell by searching the repo for `configuration.get_config().items`, S3 is the only culprit for this issue.

## Question (fbo solution)
I understand the motivation here is to be able to specify s3 boto connection options via the Luigi config, but i'm not aware of a best strategy to limit either a list of approved keys to keep or if there's a way for the ConfigParser to parse and interpolate only those keys within the explicit `[s3]` path (ignoring those in `[DEFAULT]`).

I'm happy to tackle this, but suggestions are appreciated and welcome.

## Source Code at Fault
```python
class S3Client(FileSystem):
    _s3 = None 
    
    def __init__(self, aws_access_key_id=None, aws_secret_access_key=None, **kwargs):
        from boto.s3.key import Key
        options = self._get_s3_config()  # pulls all s3 section vars, including DEFAULT
        options.update(kwargs)
        if aws_access_key_id:
            options['aws_access_key_id'] = aws_access_key_id
        if aws_secret_access_key:
            options['aws_secret_access_key'] = aws_secret_access_key
        
        self.Key = Key
        self._options = options  # all s3 and DEFAULT key/value pairs

    @property
    def s3(self):
        # only import boto when needed to allow top-lvl s3 module import
        import boto
        import boto.s3.connection

        options = dict(self._options)  # var of interest

        if self._s3:
            return self._s3

        aws_access_key_id = options.get('aws_access_key_id')
        aws_secret_access_key = options.get('aws_secret_access_key')

        # Removing key args would break backwards compability
        role_arn = options.get('aws_role_arn')
        role_session_name = options.get('aws_role_session_name')

        aws_session_token = None

        if role_arn and role_session_name:
            from boto import sts

            sts_client = sts.STSConnection()
            assumed_role = sts_client.assume_role(role_arn, role_session_name)
            aws_secret_access_key = assumed_role.credentials.secret_key
            aws_access_key_id = assumed_role.credentials.access_key
            aws_session_token = assumed_role.credentials.session_token

        for key in ['aws_access_key_id', 'aws_secret_access_key', 'aws_role_session_name', 'aws_role_arn']:
            if key in options:
                options.pop(key)
        self._s3 = boto.s3.connection.S3Connection(aws_access_key_id,
                                                   aws_secret_access_key,
                                                   security_token=aws_session_token,
                                                   **options)  # passes all key/value pairs (except auth) to boto - may cause error
        return self._s3
...

    def _get_s3_config(self, key=None):
        try:
            config = dict(configuration.get_config().items('s3'))  # pulls all k/v from s3 and DEFAULT
        except NoSectionError:
            return {}
        # So what ports etc can be read without us having to specify all dtypes
        for k, v in six.iteritems(config):
            try:
                config[k] = int(v)
            except ValueError:
                pass
        if key:
            return config.get(key)
        return config
...
```

issue_title: Accept hyperlink.URLs
issue_body:
You can't pass `treq` a `URL` because of how `twisted.web.client.Agent` works:

```python
import hyperlink, treq, twisted.internet.task

@twisted.internet.task.react
def run(reactor):
    return treq.get(hyperlink.URL.from_text(u"https://twistedmatrix.com/"))
```

```
  Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/tmp/example.py", line 3, in <module>
    @twisted.internet.task.react
  File "/home/mrw/.virtualenvs/tmp-963c1ecd0f4cfd34/lib/python2.7/site-packages/twisted/internet/task.py", line 908, in react
    finished = main(_reactor, *argv)
  File "/tmp/example.py", line 5, in run
    return treq.get(hyperlink.URL.from_text(u"https://twistedmatrix.com/"))
  File "/home/mrw/.virtualenvs/tmp-963c1ecd0f4cfd34/lib/python2.7/site-packages/treq/api.py", line 24, in get
    return _client(**kwargs).get(url, headers=headers, **kwargs)
  File "/home/mrw/.virtualenvs/tmp-963c1ecd0f4cfd34/lib/python2.7/site-packages/treq/client.py", line 109, in get
    return self.request('GET', url, **kwargs)
  File "/home/mrw/.virtualenvs/tmp-963c1ecd0f4cfd34/lib/python2.7/site-packages/treq/client.py", line 218, in request
    bodyProducer=bodyProducer)
  File "/home/mrw/.virtualenvs/tmp-963c1ecd0f4cfd34/lib/python2.7/site-packages/twisted/web/client.py", line 1973, in request
    deferred = self._agent.request(method, uri, headers, bodyProducer)
  File "/home/mrw/.virtualenvs/tmp-963c1ecd0f4cfd34/lib/python2.7/site-packages/twisted/web/client.py", line 2041, in request
    deferred = self._agent.request(method, uri, headers, bodyProducer)
  File "/home/mrw/.virtualenvs/tmp-963c1ecd0f4cfd34/lib/python2.7/site-packages/twisted/web/client.py", line 1838, in request
    lastRequest = _FakeUrllib2Request(uri)
  File "/home/mrw/.virtualenvs/tmp-963c1ecd0f4cfd34/lib/python2.7/site-packages/twisted/web/client.py", line 1720, in __init__
    self.uri = nativeString(uri)
  File "/home/mrw/.virtualenvs/tmp-963c1ecd0f4cfd34/lib/python2.7/site-packages/twisted/python/compat.py", line 412, in nativeString
    raise TypeError("%r is neither bytes nor unicode" % s)
TypeError: URL.from_text(u'https://twistedmatrix.com/') is neither bytes nor unicode
```

https://github.com/python-hyper/hyperlink/pull/55 means we can call `bytes` on `URL`s to get something we can pass `Agent.request`.

On the one hand, calling `bytes` on any old object seems bad, but on the other, we already [use `URL` to convert `unicode` URLs to `bytes`](https://github.com/twisted/treq/blob/release-17.8.0/src/treq/client.py#L135-L136).

If there's any API change, I favor adding an `else` that does `url = bytes(url)`, but we'd also need to fix `_combine_query_params`.

However, I think I prefer that `treq` only accept `bytes` and `unicode`, and that we tell people to do `bytes(theirURL)` themselves.

issue_title: Python releases exist that contain no code changes
issue_body:
This is a bug in the release process. It looks like releases get created as one atomic unit. This leads to a situation where there are releases of individual component libraries that don't actually contain any code changes (and nor do their parents), leaving downstream users to resolve version conflicts that don't actually exist.

For example, twitter.common.quantity==0.32.0 and twitter.common.quantity==0.33.0 have identical code, but the twitter.common.quantity==0.33.0 requires twitter.common.lang==0.33.0, so the metadata is different. Okay, that warrants releasing a new version. But wait, twitter.common.lang==0.32.0 and twitter.common.lang==0.33.0 have identical code and no dependencies. So twitter.common.lang's version should not have been incremented, and that increment should not have cascaded to its dependees.

The alternative: a version change for a component needs to "cascade" to its dependees, but only when there's an actual code change. This means that a change to twitter.common.quantity source files causes an increment to its version and its dependees, but not to its parent twitter.common.lang.

I'm not sure how the version strategy is implemented currently but would like to start a discussion toward changing it before the next release.

issue_title: # of iterations in Benchmark mode should be adaptive or configurable
issue_body: In 0.6.4, 100 iterations are done on Benchmark mode independent of problem kind and size. This can lead to very (too) short or very (too) long execution times. issue_body:
I'm seeing a case where MBQI produces instances that are true in the model, which leads to an infinite loop generating the same instances. Unfortunately, I can't reproduce this in an smt2 file. 

I'm printing both the model and the instances in stm_model_checker.cpp. The output looks like this:

MODEL_CHECKER INVOKED
model:

...

(define __ts0_a true)

...

(define __prm:V0 nodeord.t!val!1)

...

(define (server.impl.current_inst x!0)
  (if (= x!0 nodeord.t!val!1) (configarr.end (server.impl.history nodeord.t!val!1))
    (__server.impl.current_inst x!0)))

...

(smt.mbqi "started")
(smt.mbqi :failed null)
(smt.mbqi :failed null)
(smt.mbqi :num-failures 2)
(assert (let ((a!1 (ite __ts0_a
                (ite (= |__prm:V0| |__prm:V0|)

                (__server.impl.current_inst |__prm:V0|))))

...

The last formula is one of the added instances (the one quantifier has been instantiated with |__prm:V0|). If you plug in the model values for __ts0_a and |__prm:V0| and simplify the outer ite, you see that it exactly matches the definition of server.impl.current_inst in the model, so this formula should evaluate to true in the model. One thing I noticed is that removing the outer ite on condition __tso_a makes this bug go away, so the bug might be related to the ite. 

Any thoughts on where to start looking for this problem?

Thanks -- Ken

issue_title: Sensible defaults for tzinfos
issue_body:
So we don't want to hard-code tzinfos, but I claim that the following or something like it would be a Sensible Default for tzinfos (at least for US users)

```
tzinfos_us = {
    'PST': dateutil.tz.gettz('US/Pacific'),
    'PDT': dateutil.tz.gettz('US/Pacific'),
    'PT': dateutil.tz.gettz('US/Pacific'),
    'MST': dateutil.tz.gettz('US/Mountain'),
    'MDT': dateutil.tz.gettz('US/Mountain'),
    'MT': dateutil.tz.gettz('US/Mountain'),
    'CST': dateutil.tz.gettz('US/Central'),
    'CDT': dateutil.tz.gettz('US/Central'),
    'CT': dateutil.tz.gettz('US/Central'),
    'EST': dateutil.tz.gettz('US/Eastern'),
    'EDT': dateutil.tz.gettz('US/Eastern'),
    'ET': dateutil.tz.gettz('US/Eastern')}
```

There are others that are pretty close to unambiguous: BRST, CEST, ...  If this would make it easier for a large fraction of users to Do It Right, then we should consider providing a shortcut to something like this.

issue_title: Easily compress/decompress itsdb profiles
issue_body:
PyDelphin has no trouble reading or writing compressed (.gz) files in [incr tsdb()] profiles, so a nice feature for the `delphin` command would be to compress a profile in-place. Perhaps this could be part of the `mkprof` subcommand. This could be done just by adding an `--in-place` switch to alternate with the otherwise required `--source`:

```
delphin mkprof DEST (--source PROFILE | --in-place)
                    [--apply=APL]... [--filter=CND]...
                    [--relations=FILE] [--full|--skeleton] [--gzip]
                    [-v...|-q]
```

Then to compress:

```
delphin mkprof path/to/profile --in-place --full --gzip
```

To decompress:

```
delphin mkprof path/to/profile --in-place --full
```

But this could also be simpler, e.g. something like:

```
delphin mkprof path/to/profile --compress
delphin mkprof path/to/profile --decompress
```

But then we'd need a new usage synposis and 2 new options, instead of just one new option.

issue_title: Display errors from server, even if not json-formatted
issue_body:
### What was wrong?

In cases like #145 the error messages sent back from the server are not displayed (specifically, when the error message is not formatted as json). Instead, the provider raises a `TimeoutError`, ignoring any "partial" response received from the server.

That response might be something very helpful, like `"Provided Host header is not whitelisted.\n"`. Without that info, the user is blind to the problem that the node is complaining about.

### How can it be fixed?

- [ ] create sub-issues for IPC, WS, and HTTP providers
- [ ] resolve in IPC, either by adding a method to `Timeout` like `set_message_to_raise_on_timeout()` and add the partial response there, or by catching and re-raising the error with the partial response
- [ ] resolve in HTTP, maybe by changing `requests` to use a streaming response
- [ ] resolve in WS

issue_title: Is there a standard stop sign?
issue_body:
Hello,
I am pretty new to serial communication, so please excuse my noob question but is there a standard "stop sign" at the end of a write command that gets added?
My problem: 
I have a rf-device connected to my raspberry pi via uart and want to communicate with it via Python 3.4 and pyserial. So i send a byte string like b'\x02\x06\xf1\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00' for example which does what it's supposed to do. But the answer from the rf-device is always that the expected pause between telegrams was not over 10ms. 
The rf-device does not have a stop sign like "\x00" to determine the end of a command because all hex values from 00 to FF are possible. Every command that i send has a start sign(\x02) and then the length of the command so it knows when to expect a new command. But between commands there has to be a minimum pause of 10ms.
The data that i send seems to be correct(because the rf-device sends the right command to my rf-reciever) but i still get the answer that the pause was not respected, so i asked myself if the write function of pyserial adds a stop sign like \x00 at the end of the data it sends.
Code that i use to send:
`port = serial.Serial(device="/dev/serial0", baudrate=9600, timeout=15.0)
port.write(b'\x02\x06\xf1\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00')`
RF-Device communication:
9600 Baud
8 Bytes
No Parity
1 Stopbit

Sorry for the long post, but i hope i could explain my problem and somebody can help me out.

Thanks in advance

issue_title: non-compact ROIs should be over-segmented, then merged back?
issue_body:
How should we support non-localized ROIs, like long dendrites, or axons with multiple boutons? 

One argument is they *should* be split up into pieces, since different segments might respond differently. We can then have a posthoc step to merge them back, similar to [this]('http://www.billconnelly.net/?p=471'}) Matlab implementation. The posthoc step might require proximity and high correlation, like issue #28 . 

We could use the GUI protocol for merging ROIs, so that users can also un-merge them in the GUI as proposed in issue #17 . We can still use the binary tree procedure to merge sequentially, since this will at most double the number of ROIs, and these types of fields of view will be sparse anyway.

def parse_args():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Test the person classifier')

    parser.add_argument('--tfrecords', dest='tfrecords',
                        help='Paths to tfrecords.', type=str,
                        nargs='+', required=True)

    parser.add_argument('--checkpoint_path', dest='checkpoint_path',
                        help='Path to a specific model to test against. If a directory, then the newest checkpoint file will be used.',
                        type=str,
                        required=True, default=None)

    parser.add_argument('--save_dir', dest='savedir',
                        help='Path to directory to store summary files.', type=str,
                        required=True)

    parser.add_argument('--config', dest='config_file',
                        help='Path to the configuration file.',
                        required=True, type=str)

    parser.add_argument('--eval_interval_secs', dest='eval_interval_secs',
                        help='Go into an evaluation loop, waiting this many seconds between evaluations. Default is to evaluate once.',
                        required=False, type=int, default=0)

    parser.add_argument('--batch_size', dest='batch_size',
                        help='The number of images in a batch.',
                        required=False, type=int, default=None)

    parser.add_argument('--batches', dest='batches',
                        help='Maximum number of iterations to run. Default is all records (modulo the batch size).',
                        required=False, type=int, default=0)

    parser.add_argument('--model_name', dest='model_name',
                        help='The name of the architecture to use.',
                        required=False, type=str, default=None)

    parser.add_argument('--read_images', dest='read_images',
                        help='Read the images from the file system using the `filename` field rather than using the `encoded` field of the tfrecord.',
                        action='store_true', default=False)

    args = parser.parse_args()
    return args


def main():
    args = parse_args()

    cfg = parse_config_file(args.config_file)

    if args.batch_size != None:
        cfg.BATCH_SIZE = args.batch_size

    if args.model_name != None:
        cfg.MODEL_NAME = args.model_name

    test(
        tfrecords=args.tfrecords,
        checkpoint_path=args.checkpoint_path,
        save_dir=args.savedir,
        max_iterations=args.batches,
        eval_interval_secs=args.eval_interval_secs,
        cfg=cfg,
        read_images=args.read_images
    )


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

`

issue_title: the road to splinter 1.0
issue_body:
#  the road to splinter 1.0

splinter has 8 years but it's still in a minor version. :(

This issue is a way to share a plan with all everyone that uses and contributes with splinter to make the
1.0 release and start following the [semantic versioning](https://semver.org/) afterwards.

The idea is to stabilize the API on 1.0 version keeping the python 2 and 3 support and drop python 2 support on 2.0 version.

## TODO list for splinter 1.0:

- fix critial bugs and inconsistencies like (issue #509)
- improve docs coverage
- remove phantomjs support and improve firefox/chrome headless tests and docs (we should release a deprecation warning for phantomjs in 0.8, and remove it on 0.9.)
- support for python 2 and 3
- add deprecation warning for python 2
- stabilize tests

## splinter 2.0

- drop support for python 2

What do you think about it?

After this we will create a Milestone with all the issues discussed here to wrap up the discussion.

issue_title: Response with query string, but match_querystring=False should raise a warning
issue_body:
## Problem
I've debugged an unrelated part of the an application for hours before noticing that mocked responses were matched unexpectedly, due to me not seeing the default `match_querystring=False`. A naive test case might do something like this:

```py
$ python
Python 3.6.6 (default, Jul 19 2018, 14:25:17) 
[GCC 8.1.1 20180712 (Red Hat 8.1.1-5)] on linux
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import responses
>>> import requests
>>> 
>>> responses.start()
>>> 
>>> responses.add(responses.GET, "https://example.com/api?endpoint=1",
...     json={'endpoint': 1}
... )
>>> responses.add(responses.GET, "https://example.com/api?endpoint=2",
...     json={'endpoint': 2}
... )
>>> 
>>> requests.get('https://example.com/api?endpoint=2').content
b'{"endpoint": 1}'
>>> requests.get('https://example.com/api?endpoint=1').content
b'{"endpoint": 2}'
>>> 
```

## Proposal
While I'd prefer the default for `match_querystring` to be changed, I'd propose responses throws a `RuntimeWarning` when a response is added that contains a query string without setting `match_querystring` explicitly.

issue_title: V0.3.2 版本正式发布啦！！！
issue_body:
# pyecharts V0.3.2 发布日志

> pyecharts V0.3.2 发布，这是一个重大更新的版本。

## 1 全面重构地图加载方式

从本版本开始，为了缩减项目本身的体积以及维持 pyecharts 项目的轻量化运行，pyecharts 将不再自带地图 js 文件。如用户需要用到地图图表，可自行安装对应的地图文件包。下面介绍如何安装。

地图文件被分成了三个 Python 包，分别为

1. [全球国家地图](https://echarts-maps.github.io/echarts-countries-js/): [echarts-countries-pypkg](https://github.com/pyecharts/echarts-countries-pypkg) (1.9MB)
2. [中国省级地图](https://echarts-maps.github.io/echarts-china-provinces-js/): [echarts-china-provinces-pypkg](https://github.com/pyecharts/echarts-china-provinces-pypkg) (730KB)
3. [中国市级地图](https://echarts-maps.github.io/echarts-china-cities-js/): [echarts-china-cities-pypkg](https://github.com/pyecharts/echarts-china-cities-pypkg) (3.8MB)

安装方式也很简单，可以仅选择所需要的包，也可以全部安装
```
$ pip install echarts-countries-pypkg
$ pip install echarts-china-provinces-pypkg
$ pip install echarts-china-cities-pypkg
```

地图包由 [lml](https://github.com/chfw/lml) 负责管理和识别， lml 是一个松散的包管理工具，有兴趣的同学可以前往了解。三个包也同时会将 js 文件带入到 notebook 环境中去。


## 2 恢复 online 函数的使用

由于考虑到不少用户有将 notebook 保存为 html 文件的做法，所以恢复 online 函数的使用，使用户 .html 即使脱离 notebook 环境也可以挂载到 Github host，确保图形可以正常显示。


## 3 为 Kline 提供 Candlestick 别名

现在可以引入 Candlestick 类
```
from pyecharts import Candlestick
candlestick = Candlestick("K 线图-自定义标记点风格")
candlestick.add("日K", DATE, data, mark_point=["min", "max"],
                mark_point_symbolsize=80,
                datazoom_orient='vertical',
                mark_line_valuedim=['lowest', 'highest'])
candlestick.render()
```

## 4 加入 AppVeyor CI

一直以来 pyecharts 都只有 Travis CI，而 Travis 又只是针对 Linux 测试，现在加入了 AppVeyor CI，提供了 Windows 平台的测试功能。

## 5 pyecharts/assets 

自本版本开始，将不再为 Flask/Django/.. 等 WEB 框架提供 DEFAULT_HOST，但我们为开发者新建了 pyecharts/assets 库挂载 pyecharts 所需要的所有 js 文件，可以使用下面语句替换原来的 DEFAULT_HOST
```
REMOTE_HOST = "https://pyecharts.github.io/assets/js"
```


## 5 修复了若干 BUG
* [issue#322](https://github.com/pyecharts/pyecharts/issues/322) 修复在 timeline 中不能设置多个 legend 的 bug
* [issue#357](https://github.com/pyecharts/pyecharts/issues/357) 修复 Line 图 symbol 大小不能调整的 bug
* [issue#371](https://github.com/pyecharts/pyecharts/issues/371) 修复 Parallel 图 Line 样式失效的 bug
* [issue#378](https://github.com/pyecharts/pyecharts/issues/378) 修复 Geo 图中当多次 render 时相同 value 值会被叠加的 bug
* [issue#338](https://github.com/pyecharts/pyecharts/issues/338) 修复 timeline 中 map 的 visualmap 组件不能正常显示的 bug

更多细节内容可移步至 [changelog](http://pyecharts.org/#/zh-cn/changelog) 做进一步了解

issue_title: Send email through smtp in superlance using crashmail
issue_body:
Hi,

I've posted this question on stackoverflow but given there isn't a lot of questions about superlance, I decided to repost the question here.

I'm trying to set up the email sending when a process changes state in [supervisord][1] by using [crashmail][2]. Having no luck with the default `sendmail` program which requires quite a lot of setup, I decided to go with a small script in Python that sends email using SMTP.

This worked very well (I received indeed an email saying that the process state changes) for the first state change but stop working afterward. I have tried to change different options in `supervisord` such as `buffer_size` or `autorestart` but it has no effect.

Here is the script I use to trigger the `supervisord` state changes:

    import time

    from datetime import datetime

    if __name__ == '__main__':
        print(">>>>> STARTING ...", flush=True)
        while True:
            print("sleep now:", datetime.utcnow(), flush=True)
            time.sleep(30)
            raise Exception("meo meo")

This is the script that sends email through Gmail. This one will send the `stdin`.

    #!/usr/bin/env python
    
    import smtplib
    
    
    def get_server():
        smtpserver = smtplib.SMTP('smtp.gmail.com:587')
        smtpserver.ehlo()
        smtpserver.starttls()
        smtpserver.login("user@gmail.com", "password")
        return smtpserver
    
    
    if __name__ == '__main__':
        import sys
    
        data = sys.stdin.read()
    
        s = get_server()
        s.sendmail('from@gmail.com', ['myemail@fitle.com'], data)
        s.quit()
   
Here is my `supervisord.conf`

	[eventlistener:crashmail]
	command=crashmail -a -m myemail@gmail.com -s /home/ubuntu/mysendmail.py
	events=PROCESS_STATE
	buffer_size=102400
	autorestart=true


Does anyone have any idea why? 
Thanks!


  [1]: http://supervisord.org/
  [2]: https://superlance.readthedocs.org/en/latest/crashmail.html

issue_title: Replace Column.get_default_order_is_ascending() with something more readable and easier to grasp
issue_body:
The current boolean or None situation makes for hard to understand code. We should create a new method, ``get_default_ordering()``, and deprecate ``get_default_order_is_ascending()``. The new method should return ``"asc"``, ``"desc"`` or ``None``. This will make both of these examples fairly obvious:

Do not order the column by default:
```python
class MyColumn(objecttable.PlainTextColumn):
    modelfield = "title"
    def get_default_ordering(self):
        return None
```

Order ascending by default
```python
class MyColumn(objecttable.PlainTextColumn):
    modelfield = "title"
    def get_default_ordering(self):
        return "asc"
```

issue_title: Setting the BigchainDB nodes attribute propagates wherever relevant
issue_body:
Changing, (whether it is via a setter, or directly) the `nodes` attribute on a BigchainDB instance will cause the `transport` attribute to be out of sync with the change. Briefly, the change need to be applied to:

`BigchainDB --> transport --> pool of connections`
### Notes
- This issue is more concerned about consistency
- One way could be to remove the `_nodes` attribute

issue_title: Sending amount of 0 sends all bitcoins
issue_body:
If I supply a value of 0 to the api endpoint for sending balance, it sends my WHOLE balance to the endpoint. 

I understand that we have undocumented functionality where if you don't supply the `amount` argument, that the entire balance will be sent (we use this functionality to send the whole balance from the core wallet to the browser wallet). However, I would expect that if I supply an amount that that amount would be used. Supplying 0 or some value that's too small to be used to make a transaction should be a `400` error. 

This is a pretty big bug - someone could easily inadvertently send their entire wallet balance to the wrong person because of this. I'll add some additional error checking on the browser UI, but we should also make sure that the API currently interprets 0 as 0 instead as "send all" as well.

issue_title: template_generator invokes pkgutil.walk_packages incorrectly.
issue_body:
`template_generator.py` incorrectly invokes `pkgutil.walk_packages` here:

https://github.com/cloudtools/troposphere/blob/master/troposphere/template_generator.py#L313

https://docs.python.org/3/library/pkgutil.html

`walk_packages` expects a `list` rather than a string, of file paths. This error is not caught by the tests because if a string is passed in, then the entire string is treated as a list of paths. The only valid path that does anything will be the `'.'` in `'.py'`, resulting in the current directory being searched for directories named `'troposphere'`. Obviously, in the test environment, it will find such a package.

issue_title: RDS master password of any length is passable to troposphere
issue_body:
troposphere allows any password length to be accepted. The current requirements are as follows:
```
The password for the master database user can be any printable ASCII character except "/", """, or "@". Master password constraints differ for each database engine.

MySQL, Amazon Aurora, and MariaDB

Must contain 8 to 41 characters.

Oracle

Must contain 8 to 30 characters.

SQL Server

Must contain 8 to 128 characters.

PostgreSQL

Must contain 8 to 128 characters.```

issue_title: Maintenance / my role in this project
issue_body:
I seem to be the most active maintainer of this project at the moment,
which is fine by me, but as I'm hesitant to merge my own PRs (as this
isn't my project and I feel for community projects such as this, whoever
works on a PR shouldn't also be merging it), bugs get fixed very slowly.
New features, especially backwards compatibility breaking ones obviously
get merged even slower, if they are getting worked on at all.

If nobody has time to review at least the bug fixing PRs, it would
perhaps be better if I did at least merge bug fixing PRs (and you don't
shout too loudly at me if I break anything)?

Other maintainers, what are your opinions?

issue_title: Issue with gaudi options parsing
issue_body:
An issue was noticed by a few users that the newest DaVinci (v39r0p1) does not work with ganga. 

To reproduce it is enough to do:
```python
app = DaVinci(version='v39r0p1')
app.extraopts = 'from Configurables import DaVinci; DaVinci(DataType="2015")'
app.prepare()
```

I think the issue is with the way ganga tries to read the pickled option file at
https://github.com/ganga-devs/ganga/blob/0b796886d15f843d38e1a85bbb6078f0677dca93/python/GangaLHCb/Lib/Applications/PythonOptionsParser.py#L66

The problem is that gaudirun.py is not called in the correct environment (DaVinci), thus reading of the pickle fails since various classes are not available. See this little demo outside ganga:

```sh
lb-run DaVinci v39r0p1 gaudirun.py --option "from Configurables import DaVinci; DaVinci(DataType='2015')" --output test.pkl -n
# the following fails as in ganga
python -c "import cPickle; cPickle.load(file('test.pkl'))"
# this one works
lb-run DaVinci v39r0p1 python -c "import cPickle; cPickle.load(file('test.pkl'))"
```

issue_title: Move to IPython 3.x by default
issue_body:
I would like to see us move to IPython 3.x by default by 6.1.13.

I think this is as working as the presently default IPython 0.6 implementation we're using.

It would be nice to drop the old IPython 0.6 related code once we've settled on IPython 3 as it's unlikely we will have anyone dropping back to an older version any time soon.

I would push for it for 6.1.12 but there have been a lot of changes in Core which I'd like to see tested and released first and whilst IPython shouldn't interfere with this I'd rather not risk too many things changing under the hood in 6.1.12.

Does anyone have a strong objection to this move?

Thanks,

Rob

issue_title: Test harness FED 9.2.13 fails
issue_body:
**Observation:** The parent of the dut sends a icmp destination unreachable.

**Cause:** the ping destination is the mleid of the dut, but the mesh header final destination is the dut's parent's rloc16. Because the dut is a FED, the parent does not know the dut's mleid, therefore sending the icmp error (it can not ip forward the message).

**Down the rabbit hole:** the Final destination in the mesh header is the dut's parent's rloc16. I believe this should be the dut's rloc16. The preceding energy request is supposed to have the same source and destination as the ping, but it hasn't. On inspection, the mesh header of the energy request has as final destination the dut's rloc16. So why is the addressing of the energy request and the ping request different? I believe the answer lies in the energy response. The dut sends this response to its parent (_ip route over_). The parent then forwards (_mesh under_) it to the commissioner (*). The commissioner sees in the mesh header the dut's parent's rloc16 associated with the dut's mleid. I think the commissioner updates its eid-to-rloc cache with this (incorrect) information. Consequentially, it uses this incorrect information to send the ping request.
Attached are the wireshark and test harness logs
[logging.zip](https://github.com/openthread/openthread/files/1047563/logging.zip)


(*) I think this behaviour is incorrect: the dut should use a mesh header with O=dut's rloc16 and F=commissioner's rloc16

warning: No processor claimed any of these annotations: com.squareup.multipass.client.webservice.CsrfToken,javax.ws.rs.Produces,com.squareup.sake.rpc.annotation.ServerPort,com.squareup.config.AppName,com.google.inject.Inject,javax.ws.rs.Path,com.squareup.webservice.security.annotations.AllowInsecure,com.squareup.service.container.rpc.annotation.ConsumedServices,com.squareup.webservice.security.annotations.AllowUnauthorized,com.squareup.service.container.webservice.annotation.ExposedWebResources,javax.ws.rs.Consumes,com.squareup.config.ConfigValue,com.squareup.service.container.Application,javax.ws.rs.HeaderParam,com.squareup.service.container.rpc.annotation.Incoming,javax.ws.rs.core.Context,org.junit.experimental.categories.Category,com.squareup.service.container.components.Component,com.squareup.service.container.rpc.annotation.CurrentUser,com.squareup.sake.rpc.annotation.CallHandlerMap,com.squareup.service.container.cronjobs.annotations.CronJobs,javax.ws.rs.PathParam,javax.ws.rs.GET,org.junit.runner.RunWith,javax.inject.Singleton,javax.inject.Inject,com.squareup.service.container.admin.AdminConsole,javax.inject.Qualifier,com.google.inject.servlet.RequestScoped,com.squareup.service.container.core.webservice.ServiceUriPaths,javax.annotation.Nullable,com.sun.jersey.spi.resource.Singleton,org.junit.Before,com.squareup.service.container.AdminResource,javax.ws.rs.POST,com.squareup.service.container.testing.ContainerTestRunner.App,com.squareup.service.container.rpc.server.RpcServerComponentModule.ServerConfig,org.junit.Test,com.squareup.service.container.persistence.annotation.Persistence,com.squareup.multipass.client.component.Multipass,com.google.inject.Provides,com.squareup.service.container.AppModules,com.google.common.annotations.VisibleForTesting,com.squareup.webservice.JettyThreadPool
```

So I did a spot check. There is a processor for com.squareup.service.container.Application, and it is configured.

```
$ ./pants depmap <target>
...

                internal-service.container.annotation-processors.src.main.java.app-config-processor
...
```

I see that with JMake and the twitter commons Compiler, there is a `-Tnowarnregex` option that was suppressing these before.   @stuhood reports that Twitter filed an internal bug for it.

Consensus in the group discussion is that we should do something similar in zinc that we did with jmake.  Maybe this time build something into pants to ignore certain error message output instead of passing an argument to zinc.

issue_title: allow a single Get to convert to ExecuteProcessRequest and execute the process
issue_body:
See the [fixme](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/pull/5703/files#diff-7f3f0b29ef16b071ae652a0ef434bd4fR73) left in #5703. A brief description of what this change would entail:

```python
@rule(Concatted, [Select(CatExecutionRequest)])
def cat_files_process_result_concatted(cat_exe_req):
  # this is what we're doing now:
  cat_proc_req = yield Get(ExecuteProcessRequest, CatExecutionRequest, cat_exe_req)
  cat_process_result = yield Get(ExecuteProcessResult, ExecuteProcessRequest, cat_proc_req)
  # this is what we'd like to be able to do instead:
  cat_process_result = yield Get(ExecuteProcessResult, CatExecutionRequest, cat_exe_req)
  yield Concatted(value=cat_process_result.stdout)
```

However, the below fails because [`Select#gen_nodes()` calls `ExecuteProcess::lift()` in `nodes.rs`](https://github.com/cosmicexplorer/pants/blob/7b7f557ff19eec80845a1842834da3b67c33b65f/src/rust/engine/src/nodes.rs#L315), which tries to interpret our `CatExecutionRequest` as an `ExecuteProcessRequest` (which fails).

I made [a branch which attempts to fix this](https://github.com/cosmicexplorer/pants/tree/single-get-for-process-execution) after reading @stuhood's [comment on the PR](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/pull/5703#issuecomment-381507758), but currently it ends up executing `execute_process_noop` when I try to do a single `Get(ExecuteProcessResult, CatExecutionRequest, cat_exe_req)`, which throws.

This doesn't seem like it should be a difficult change to make, I'm just finding it difficult to make progress on it so dumping it here for now.

issue_title: Abusive memory usage after high traffic multiprocess gunicorn
issue_body:
After doing some tests w/ [patator](https://github.com/lanjelot/patator) to try and guess paths on an internal app, we encountered an abusive memory usage case which was not obvious. This is kind of related to: https://github.com/prometheus/client_python/issues/275

A 4GB docker was oomkilling its processes after a DoS phase, those processes were respawned by gunicorn, who was reading at boot time the .db files, triggering a 200Mb/s disk read at each bootstrap, all those bootstraps were triggering oomkills, with a resulting endless oom loop. 

I discarded any incoming traffic, isolated the docker and saw the exact same thing. So, a strace on the active pids were showing a lot of read, I looked at the opened files and saw those .db files. After a brutal deletion of those files, I finally got the container to settle down to a normal CPU/RAM usage (forgot to mention that the CPU was stuck @500%, seems coherent with the db load).

I finally took a screenshot of the resulting variation of both CPU and RAM on ctop:
![](https://dooby.fr/y/rq69n4m)

Some tech details:

```
prometheus_client==0.0.21
gunicorn==19.8.1
```

issue_title: singleton.py misdesign
issue_body:
Hello

I've just made the same issue to one project (PyBitMessage) that using your idea. The thing I want to talk aboue is file [singleton.py](https://github.com/pycontribs/tendo/blob/master/tendo/singleton.py) with singleinstance class especially.

The design of this class is incorrect because of two things as below:
1. This class has to ensure that lock file is being deleted when app is closed (as far as I understand it). But it won't work. Well, it would, probably, but as it's stated in python doc, it's not guaranteed that `__del__` method will ever called. So it can easily lead to situation when lock file remains in system after app shutdown.
2. There is `try..except` block in `__del__` method which would not work ever because, as stated in python doc, any exception that occurs while running `__del__` method will be ignored and just printed a warning in stderr instead.

Thus, I'd suggest to either make some other method like `release()` that should be called directly or redesign this class\idea.

Thanks,
Constantine

issue_title: PyCapsule_New calls have no destructor - memory leak
issue_body:
None of the calls to `PyCapsule_New` are passing in a destructor. The result is that the various PyCapsules returned by these functions will eventually leak.

For example, calling `lz4.frame.makePrefs()` in a loop will eventually consume all memory even if the result is not bound to a variable.

issue_title: ENH: quantile vs equal interval bins
issue_body:
While using quantiles is the most straightforward choice there are use cases where it would be much more reasonable to classify the factor values using equal interval bins.

An example is a common scenario where a factor produces positive and negative values (e.g.  in -1, 1 range) and alphalens should be able to calculate statistics on two distinct group: the positive values  group and the negative values one. Currently this is not possible if the positive and negative values are not equally balances (50% of positive values and 50% of negative values).

So I am proposing to have two way of classifying the factor values:
 - Quantiles (current implementation): this method classifies factor values into a certain number of categories with an equal number of units in each category. This produces bins with the same amount of elements but with different values ranges. 
- Equal Interval bins: the range of factor values is divided equally into however many categories have been chosen (.e.g. range -1,1 and 2 categories would result in one category in the range -1,0 and a second category in the range 0,1).  This second option would produce bins with varying number of elements, but with the same values span.

The Single Euro Payments Area (SEPA) is a payment-integration initiative of the European Union for simplification of bank transfers denominated in euro.

issue_title: Most class variables are unnecessary
issue_body: A handful of the class-level variables in the project are actual class-level configuration, but most of them are shadowed by the `__init__` methods of their classes. This is untidy and could lead to confusion. I'd be happy to clean them up, unless there are objections.

issue_title: Do you plan to http client?
Use scoped enums in C++ and flatbuffers.
issue_body: We should replace all instances of `enum` with `enum class`. Also, we should generate scoped enums from flatbuffers using `--scoped-enums` (see https://github.com/google/flatbuffers/blob/master/docs/source/Compiler.md).

issue_title: Fatal error in actor test.
issue_body:
I can't reproduce it reliably, but occasionally I can cause `testInheritActorFromClass` in `actor_test.py` to fail by running the following.

```
for i in {1..500}; do python test/actor_test.py ActorInheritance.testInheritActorFromClass; done
```

I have seen it fail with a check failure in line 504 in `local_scheduler.cc`, which is

```
CHECK(ActorID_equal(TaskSpec_actor_id(spec), NIL_ACTOR_ID));
```

Object reconstruction should be suppressed before it gets to this point. In this particular test, no objects should be evicted and no processes should be failing.

It is a little unclear what is happening here.

issue_title: Connecting to a different node with XRay
issue_body:
There are two issues I've been running into so far when trying to connect two raylets from different nodes:

1. The local_endpoint from https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/6e06a9e338e1045fa0ba73b366bb78a2c7f0fef8/src/ray/raylet/raylet.cc#L60 refers to a local IP address (in my case 0.0.0.0) which cannot be accessed remotely. We need to add a public IP address into redis. We can solve this as in the old codebase and have the node IP address passed in via Python.

2. Connections from remote nodes need to be activated for redis, from the command line this works as follows:
```
redis-cli config set protected-mode no
```
This should be done automatically.

issue_title: Include functionality to consume CopHub CSV catalogues?
issue_body:
As of 2017-09-04 the Copernicus Open Access Hub published monthly CSV files containing all products published during that time and available through the CopHub ([source](https://scihub.copernicus.eu/userguide/6CatalogueViewInCSV)).

Is this something that should be consumeable by `sentinelsat` in addition to the search of the DHuS?

Currently they are structured as 1 CSV file per month containing the following metadata:

- ID | The Universally Unique Identifier (UUID) of the product 
- Name | The product filename 
- ContentLength | The size in bytes of the .ZIP file 
- IngestionDate | The date and time of the ingestion of the product in the data hub system 
- ContentDate:Start* | The sensing start date and time of the product 
- ContentDate:End* | The sensing stop date and time of the product 
- Checksum:Algorithm | The hash function name used by the Data Hub system
- Checksum:Value | Hash value 

Using these we might be able to query large amounts of data faster if the search area is exceptionally large (continents and larger). On the other hand, no geographic relation of the datasets is stored.

Currently I lean towards not including functionality to use these CSV datasets as I can see no advantage to the usage of `sentinelsat`. I would like to have a discussion about this, maybe it is useful for people ingesting larger amounts of data (such as @valgur ?).

issue_title: evm: trace does not record terminating instructions
issue_body:
evm records the execution trace via a handler for the `did_execute_instruction` event. that event is emitted after the instruction implementation function returns. for some instructions like REVERT, the impl function never returns, as an exception is raised. this means that instruction is missed in the trace.

https://github.com/trailofbits/manticore/blob/743d479cbf10f40e3079d56e3f26b2d1f4e34893/manticore/ethereum.py#L1033-L1035

https://github.com/trailofbits/manticore/blob/743d479cbf10f40e3079d56e3f26b2d1f4e34893/manticore/platforms/evm.py#L1301-L1317

https://github.com/trailofbits/manticore/blob/743d479cbf10f40e3079d56e3f26b2d1f4e34893/manticore/platforms/evm.py#L1799-L1801

issue_title: Redis hook interface should exactly match StrictRedis
issue_body:
The StrictRedis client's [interface](https://github.com/andymccurdy/redis-py/blob/master/redis/client.py#L1130) for `set` is:
`set(self, name, value, ex=None, px=None, nx=False, xx=False)`

We should match that in the client hook to support the case when clients don't specify the keyword argument. There was a nasty bug in production since we was invoking the Redis client like so:
`set('foo', 'bar', 10)` instead of `set('foo', 'bar', ex=10)`. While it may be good practice to always specify the kwarg, we should still support the ability to only use positional args.

issue_title: TopologyException happens while loading data for a polygon region
issue_body:
### Background

I'm using [agdc_statistics](https://github.com/GeoscienceAustralia/agdc_statistics) to process data from all of time for polygonal regions around Australia.

### Expected behaviour
The `datacube.utils.geometry.unary_union()` function is being used to `Union` the `valid_data` polygons from my source datasets (~1900), it should return a valid polygon for all of the sources.

The relevant line of code is:

`sources_union = geometry.unary_union(source.extent.to_crs(geobox.crs) for source in sources)`

### Actual behaviour
A `TopologyException` is raised instead of a valid shape being returned.

`ERROR 1: TopologyException: found non-noded intersection between LINESTRING (1.7e+06 -2.20922e+06, 1.7e+06 -2.23041e+06)
 and LINESTRING (1.7e+06 -2.3e+06, 1.7e+06 -2.2087e+06) at 1699999.9999999993 -2210399.5855833939`



### Steps to reproduce the behaviour
Perform a unary union on the valid data regions from these dataset ids: [datasetids.txt](https://github.com/opendatacube/datacube-core/files/1037347/datasetids.txt)

OR

Perform a unary union on these geometries: [source_geoms_wkt.txt](https://github.com/opendatacube/datacube-core/files/1037351/source_geoms_wkt.txt)

The exception occurs at [geometry.py#L644](https://github.com/opendatacube/datacube-core/blob/develop/datacube/utils/geometry.py#L644) which is a call to the underlying `ogr` library.

`union = geom.UnionCascaded()`


### Environment information

* Which ``datacube --version`` are you using?
    1.3.2 
* What datacube deployment/enviornment are you running against?
    Running on the NCI, loaded from `agdc-py3-prod`

issue_title: Collect and Use Git Revision
issue_body:
Use commit ``git rev-parse HEAD`` + ``if `git diff --quiet` then '-dirty' else ''`` to capture git information at ``shed_upload``. At first use this information in the commit message to HG, but longer term we should collect the data on the tool shed in a more semantically useful way.

See also #169.

1. Succeeding tests!
2. Changed something -> more succeeding tests.
2. Remove redundant verbosity flag in tests to clean up output. -> Testing fails on python 3.5 but builds on 2.7
3. Change some characters in `echo` statements to upper case -> Testing fails on python 2.7 but builds on 3.5

issue_title: Usability
issue_body:
Have you considered any simplifications or changes to the workflow? Currently, the minimum input is this:
```
scour -i /path/to/file.svg -o /path/to/newfile.svg
```

However, would it not be possible to imply the `-i` and `-o` by default? Like this:
```
scour /path/to/file.svg /path/to/newfile.svg
```

In addition and/or alternatively, why not make `-o` optional and automatically write the new file to the same directory, appended with a number, date and/or keyword to the file?
```
scour /path/to/file.svg
```

Ideally, I just want to type `scour` and drag and drop into the CLI. Moreover, I would actually like to see an option for scrubbing multiple files at once.
```
scour /path/to/file.svg /path/to/file2.svg /path/to/file3.svg
```

Any thoughts?

issue_title: Fields with numbers in names do not capitalize correctly
issue_body:
I noticed that the field with names containing numbers (e.g. `field_i18n`) doesn't capitalize correctly.

For example:
`correct_field` becomes `correctField`, but `field_i18n` becomes `field_I18N` and `t1e2s3t` becomes `t1E2S3T` which is obvious incorrect.

This caused by using `.title` method in `str_converter.py` instead of `.capitalize`. Title method is suitable for use with words, but field names are single word.

issue_title: Use constants for channel state values
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Right now, but even more so after #413, we're using strings to check for certain channel states in many places. Instead of relying on local string values in all those places it sounds more sensible to introduce class attributes for the `Channel` class. This will prevent bugs introduced by mismatches between `close` and `closed`, as well as typos (`seltted` vs `settled`).

## TODO
- [ ] introduce class attributes in `channel.py::Channel` for `STATE_OPENED`, `STATE_CLOSED`, `STATE_SETTLED`
- [ ] replace all literal uses of channel state strings (`'open'`, `'opened'`, `'closed'`, `'settled'`) with the class attributes.

issue_title: Tickets table: dynamic table sorting overrides query sort order?
issue_body: Forgive me if is obvious, but I searched the other tickets carefully and did not see this reported. It looks like the new dynamic sorting on the Tickets table (provided by dataTables?) overrides the sort order specified by the Ticket Query. The Tickets are always sorted by ascending pk, regardless of the sort order filter. Here's a screenshot -- you can see regardless of whether the sort order is reversed, the table is always sorted by pk. As far as I can tell, this always happens. I noticed you can see this bug in the screenshots shared last year when this feature was first released: https://github.com/django-helpdesk/django-helpdesk/pull/419#issuecomment-246574490 I'm happy with the dynamic sorting - the new UI is fabulous, thank-you! But did find this confusing.

issue_title: Tornado URLSpec with URL parameters not working
issue_body:
It simply does not work with url parameters nor named url parameters?

Having the flask sample in Tornado:

```python
from apispec import APISpec
from marshmallow import Schema, fields
from tornado.web import Application, RequestHandler
from tornado.web import url
import time 
import json

# Create an APISpec
spec = APISpec(
    title='Swagger Petstore',
    version='1.0.0',
    plugins=[
        'apispec.ext.tornado',
        'apispec.ext.marshmallow',
    ],
)

# Optional marshmallow support
class CategorySchema(Schema):
    id = fields.Int()
    name = fields.Str(required=True)

class PetSchema(Schema):
    category = fields.Nested(CategorySchema, many=True)
    name = fields.Str()

class PetHandler(RequestHandler):
    def get():
        """A cute furry animal endpoint.
        ---
        get:
            description: Get a random pet
            responses:
                200:
                    description: A pet to be returned
                    schema: PetSchema
        """
        pet_dict = {
            'category': 1,
            'name': ['dog', 'cat', 'bird'][int(time.time()%3)]
        }
        return json.dumps(PetSchema().dump(pet).data)
```

If I dump the spec without parameters:

```python
# Specs

    url(r"/random/$", PetHandler, name='RandomPet')
]

# Register entities and paths

spec.definition('Pet', schema=PetSchema)

But obviously it cannot get the correct `path_args` or `path_kwargs` and it simply fails:

issue_title: Support for MethodView
issue_body:
I'm using Flask's [`MethodView`s](http://flask.pocoo.org/docs/0.11/views/#method-based-dispatching) for an API.

Basically, the idea is to have one class per endpoint and one class method for each HTTP method. The method name is the HTTP method, lowercase.

```python
from flask.views import MethodView

class UserAPI(MethodView):

    def get(self):
        """User detail view.
        ---
        get:
            responses:
                200:
                    schema: UserSchema
        """
        users = User.query.all()
        ...

    def post(self):
        """User post.
        ---
        post:
            responses:
                201:
                    schema: UserSchema
        """
        user = User.from_form_data(request.form)
        ...

app.add_url_rule('/users/', view_func=UserAPI.as_view('users'))
```

AFAIU, there is currently no support for Flask's `MethodView` or Flask's `View`, only functions are supported. Is this correct?

I think I managed to get apispec to produce the correct OpenAPI file using the following patch:

https://github.com/marshmallow-code/apispec/compare/dev...Nobatek:dev_method_views

The idea is that if the `view` is a `MethodView`, we should not use the `view`'s docstring but loop over the docstrings of each method.

Thinking of it, maybe the only difference is that this allows the user to keep each docstring in each method rather than grouping all of them at the top of the class. This can be seen as cosmetic, but I think it would be better this way.

I'd be happy to get any feedback.

If there is interest for this feature, I may clean this up, add tests and all, but before I move any further, I'd rather be sure I'm on the right tracks.

issue_title: Remove support for generators of validators
issue_body:
Currently, a `Field` can able to receive a generator of callables for its `validate` argument. Talk about overengineering! I have yet to find a case where this might be useful; it only adds complexity and unnecessary type checking.

I propose to remove support for validator generators and only support lists or tuples of validators.

This is a breaking change, so this will be in the 2.0 release.

issue_title: (De)serialization to other types
issue_body:
I'd love for Marshmallow to be more modular, for example I'm loading from `JSON` and serializing to `bencode`. It would be great if there was a simple way to do this.

It was suggested in https://github.com/marshmallow-code/marshmallow/issues/130 but the solution assumes that the source and destination encoding are the same.

issue_title: [Question] Many-to-many relationships
issue_body:
Is there a standard way to handle many-to-many object relationships with this library? Nested fields obviously won't work because they would create duplicate objects where you want just one object that is referred to by several others.

It is possible to handle this manually, for example by using some sort of unique ``key`` (or ``name`` or ``id``) field and translating lists of the object into lists of these keys, but this is kind of awkward.

I'm thinking of something like a ``Linked`` field type that works like the ``Nested`` one, except it doesn't lead to the construction of anything and only validates that the linked object exists (or eventually exists) - sorry, I don't know enough about the library to assess whether this is possible at all. In the output dictionary, the link could be represented by the method I mentioned above.

Thank you in advance for your input.

issue_title: Do I need separate schemas for create and update operations in this case?
issue_body:
I'm developing a Flask application using marshmallow-sqlalchemy, and I've run into a funny case where I think I'd like to conditionally apply a field validator.

I have `Report` and `ReportItem` models:

```
class Report(BaseModel):
    __tablename__ = 'report'

    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    report_items = db.relationship("ReportItem",
                                         back_populates="audit_report")

class ReportItem(BaseModel):
    __tablename__ = 'report_item'
    
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    report_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('report.id'),
                                nullable=False)
    report = db.relationship("Report",
                                   back_populates="report_items")
```

I also have a `ReportSchema` and a `ReportItemSchema`:

```
class ReportSchema(BaseSchema):
    class Meta(BaseSchema.Meta):
        model = Report

    report_items = ma.List(ma.Nested('ReportItemSchema'))

class ReportItemSchema(BaseSchema):
    class Meta(BaseSchema.Meta):
        model = ReportItem
        exclude = ['report']
```

I am also using [this](https://github.com/marshmallow-code/marshmallow-sqlalchemy/issues/50#issuecomment-196813642) suggestion to implement a foreign key validator:

```
def validate_fk(rel):
    if isinstance(rel, list):
        errors = [({'id' : '%s does not exist' % obj.id}) for obj in rel if not obj.has_identity()]
        if len(errors)>0:
            raise ValidationError(errors)
    else:
        if not rel.has_identity():
            raise ValidationError({'id': '%s does not exist' % rel.id})
    return True
```

I have 3 cases that I need to handle via the API:
 - dump to JSON (via GET)
 - create new report from JSON (via POST)
 - update existing report from JSON (via PUT)

Dumping is straightforward with `ReportSchema().dump()`

Creating a new report is straightforward via:
```
# clean primary keys from POSTed data

new_instance = Report()
ReportSchema().load(data, instance=new_instance)
```

Note that we can't apply the `validate_fk` validator here, as this call will be creating both the Report and its associated `ReportItems`.

When we do an update, we *do* need to validate that all of the report items exist, and the best way to do that would be to add `validate=validate_fk` to the nested field on `Report`. 

Do I need to create a separate schema that with `validate=validate_fk` on the nested items for update operations, or is there a way to conditionally apply the validator? Is there another approach I should be taking?

issue_title: reorganize namespaces/python packages/directories for consistency
issue_body:
We should have the same scoped path to various bits of code in Python, C++, and in the on-disk layout of our headers.

I propose the following (note that I don't care much about the actual names, just the consistency):
```
galsim/artifacts/....
galsim/distort/...
...
galsim/sbprofile/_sbprofile.so
galsim/meas/hsm/_hsm.so
...
include/galsim/sbprofile/<sbprofile headers>
include/galsim/meas/hsm/<hsm headers>
...
src/sbprofile/<sbprofile source>
src/meas/hsm/<hsm source>
pysrc/sbprofile/<sbprofile wrapper code>
pysrc/meas/hsm/<hsm wrapper code>
```

In addition, the SBProfile C++ code should be in namespace ```galsim::sbprofile```, and the HSM code should be in namespace ```galsim::meas::hsm```.  Of course, right now, HSM doesn't have any C++ headers, as it's just a C executable, but that would naturally be added if we convert it to a library with a Python interface.

issue_title: social_django and Gmail API
issue_body:
I'm building a tool to look into email data kind of like Unroll.me if you are familiar with it.

To start out my team and I began using the django-social-auth library and found that it could authenticate us into an account and stored the account meta data in the Django projects database.  

Next we found the Google Gmail API had some sample code here: https://developers.google.com/gmail/api/quickstart/python 

This code worked to allow access to our users Gmail data but didn't store the email or meta data in the database to authenticate the user within the Django application. 

These two approaches apparently use both the Google Plus API and the Gmail API.  Each of these has a different URL.  Is there a way to authenticate against one API and store the credential to authenticate against another?

Is there a way to marry the two so users only need to sign in once but Django can store the email/meta data in the database and we can go through emails stored in Gmail?

Thanks for any help you can offer!

issue_title: Remove django from install_requires in setup.py
issue_body:
Hi.
I'd like to propose to remove `django` package from `install_requires` in setup.py, because it successfully breaks package updates with `pip install -U django-import-export` by installing the latest available `django` version, which is not really desirable in most cases.

Most of mature packages e.g. djangorestframework provide empty `install_requires` with `install_requires=[]` to avoid any conflicts.

In my opinion, most users will have `django` installed before installing `django-import-export` and I assume that nobody would expect that the `django-import-export` package would work without `django`.

References:
- https://github.com/django-nose/django-nose/issues/239
- https://github.com/django-guardian/django-guardian/issues/423

issue_title: Project images with affine transforms in ImageOverlay plugin
issue_body:
Hi, first of all, thanks to everyone involved with the folium project for working on such a cool library.

I was looking into the ImageOverlay feature, and I was wondering if there's been any discussion about the feasibility and interest in adding support for projecting images with affine transforms.  Does anyone know?

Thanks!

    us =  CondensedNearestNeighbour()

issue_title: Add ability to create the free Discovery environment in a trial account
issue_body:
To create the freebie Discovery environment, I need to call create_environment(..., size=0).
But the SDK currently has a check that requires size of 1, 2 or 3.
It seems zero should be allowed to enable auto-deployment of example Discovery apps to trial accounts.
